title: Man dies after kayak overturns off Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 22-year-old man died after his kayak overturned in Wellfleet, Cape Cod on Saturday evening.
Emergency crews, including the Coast Guard, conducted a search using boats, a helicopter, and drones.
The man was found in critical condition and transported to Cape Cod Hospital, where he was pronounced dead.

title: Photos: Highlights from the St. Patrick’s Day Parade
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: South Boston's St. Patrick’s Day Parade began at Andrew Square with spectators lining the streets.
Participants included Scott Poce of East Bridgewater, who painted his eyebrows green for the event.
Kathy Luginbuel and Teresa Rogers from Florida joined the parade as members of the Fascinators of Tampa Bay.

title: 2 men arrested after fatal overdose on Cape Cod prompts monthslong, multi-town investigation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: William Sarnie and Andre Silvia arrested for trafficking over 200 grams of cocaine after a fatal overdose investigation.
Arrests resulted from a multi-agency investigation involving local and state police, focusing on Barnstable, Nantucket, Sandwich, and Wareham.
Sarnie pleaded not guilty and remains held without bail; Silvia awaits arraignment for similar charges in Wareham District Court.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Harry Styles hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Harry Styles hosted and performed on 'SNL' March 14, marking his second time in dual roles.
Styles' previous 'SNL' appearances include musical guest spots in 2012, 2013, 2014 with One Direction, and solo in 2017.
Ryan Gosling introduced Styles' first musical performance, 'Dance No More,' from his album 'Kiss All the Time. Disco, Occasionally.'

title: Former Boston Public Schools teacher sentenced to 10 years in prison for child exploitation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Magee Gavin, 35, former science teacher, sentenced to 10 years for child exploitation crimes.
Gavin pleaded guilty to coercion, enticement, and possession of child pornography in December 2025.
Investigators discovered 147 explicit files and Gavin communicated with minors in the US, Canada, and UK.

title: 5 unsigned free agents that would be possible fits for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Tyreek Hill remains a risk due to legal troubles and injury, but his deep threat capability interests the Patriots.
Marquise Brown, known for his speed and big play ability, could complement the Patriots' receiving corps.
Bobby Okereke and Kenneth Murray are linebacker options for the Patriots, with Okereke offering more proven production.

title: Neemias Queta dominates in Celtics win over Wizards: 7 takeaways
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Neemias Queta led Boston Celtics to victory with 24 points, 10 rebounds, and 2 blocks in a 111-100 win over the Washington Wizards.
Jayson Tatum added 20 points and a team-high 14 rebounds but struggled with 3-point shooting, hitting only 2 of 9 attempts.
The Celtics' defense contained Wizards stars Alex Sarr and Trae Young, limiting them to a combined 15 points in the game.

title: Photos: Highlights from Dropkick Murphys’ St. Patrick’s Day kickoff at MGM Music Hall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys kicked off St. Patrick's Day weekend with a concert on March 13 at MGM Music Hall.
Openers for the Dropkick Murphys concert included The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown.
The performance featured band members like Ken Casey, Kevin Rheault, and Tim Brennan engaging the crowd.

title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC made its NWSL debut in front of over 30,000 fans at Gillette Stadium.
The match ended in a 1-0 victory for Gotham FC, with eight yellow cards issued, tying an NWSL record.
Legacy FC's coach emphasized the team's aggressive playstyle, despite a red card affecting their performance.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys performed at MGM Music Hall at Fenway on March 13, celebrating their 30th anniversary and St. Patrick's Day.
The concert featured a mix of old and new tracks, political themes, and guest artists like Heather Fahey and Austin Sparkman.
The band debuted 'Citizen I.C.E.' from their upcoming EP, criticizing U.S. Immigration policies and urging 'F–k ICE!' from the stage.
